Slightly higher scores (like 0.1 or 0.2) can be justified if considering deployment risks, but given the analysis, 0 is more appropriate.\n\n---\n\n### Final assessment:\n- The code is a standard, autogenerated factory class with no malicious intent.\n- The scores assigned in the reports are reasonable, with malware = 0 and obfuscated = 0.\n- The security risk scores could be uniformly set to 0, as there's no evident vulnerability or risk in this code snippet.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary of my own estimate:**\n- **Malware score:** 0  \n- **Obfuscated score:** 0  \n- **Risk score:** 0\n\nThis aligns with the overall benign nature of the code and the thorough analysis provided in the reports.\n\n**In conclusion:**  \nAll reports are accurate in their assessment.
